name: India
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **PM MODI PERSONALLY CONDEMNED FUJAIRAH ATTACK MAY 5 — 'UNACCEPTABLE'; HIGHEST-LEVEL INDIAN GOVT STATEMENT OF CRISIS ARC.** MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal reiterated demand for 'immediate cessation of hostilities' and called for 'free and unimpeded navigation and commerce through the Strait of Hormuz.' Indian Embassy Abu Dhabi confirmed coordination with local authorities for medical care of 3 injured Indian nationals. Escalation: yesterday's MEA 'unacceptable' framing was the most pointed crisis statement; PM Modi's personal endorsement May 5 ELEVATES diplomatic posture another tier. India did NOT name Iran directly (strategic-autonomy hedge holds) but alignment with US/UAE Hormuz freedom-of-navigation language is now PM-endorsed. **Markets:** USD/INR pulled back to ₹95.13 from ₹95.36 record (-0.14% session) on Brent collapse + RBI NOP/NDF curbs + DXY weakness; ₹95 trigger BREACHED-AND-REVERSED within 24h; ₹95.50 direct-intervention threshold UNTESTED. Nifty 24,082.50 (+0.2% 1D) UNDERPERFORMED the cross-asset risk-on regime (KOSPI +6.7%, MSCI EM +2.0%, DAX +1.7%, S&P +0.8%) — Indian equities WORST POSITIONED for crisis-relief regime among major markets because: (1) AI/semis flow rotates to Korea/Taiwan/US not India IT services; (2) Dollar weakness EM-FX strength capped by RBI curbs; (3) Hormuz operationally closed despite pause = OMC stocks (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) still capped. FII CY26 outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore (~$23B+) already exceeds CY25 full-year ₹1.66T; FPI ownership ~16%, lowest in 2 decades, BELOW domestic ownership for first time; DII (SIP-driven) absorbed ~90% (~₹1.7 lakh crore YTD). India 10Y GOI ~6.90% (-10 bps area, steady). Eight-million-strong Indian Gulf diaspora exposure factor remains active — UAE airspace closure through May 11.

current.deployment_context: Indian equities UNDERPERFORM crisis-relief regime; FII CY26 outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore (~$23B+); FPI ownership ~16% (2-decade low); DII absorbed 90%; AI flows bypass India IT services; OMCs capped on Hormuz still closed

active_triggers.condition: Rupee crosses ₹95/USD — DEACTIVATED on ceasefire. Current ₹92.55. WATCHING.
India announces fuel-price pass-through OR Iran-rupee oil track restart via Chabahar within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)
